assumptions: The identified exposure is a foreign currency exposure considered in the context of IFRS 9 hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements.
The issue is whether the exposure could fall within one of the recognized hedge accounting relationship types addressed in the provided context.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies fair value hedge as one of the three top-level hedge accounting models and includes recognised assets or liabilities and firm commitments as possible hedged items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model and specifically discusses foreign currency risk on highly probable forecast intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context separately addresses hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation, including consolidated-level designation and IFRIC 16 guidance.
